commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=963f4c0473a5d997864609623fd39a70c65f46c3)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes `createBlockReplyCoalescer` to merge buffered text into media payloads and updates direct coalescer unit coverage.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +23. Total +32 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, but not by live run: current main force-flushes the coalescer before media delivery and the existing direct coalescer test expects the split behavior. I did not run tests or live Discord in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the contributor must supply real Discord proof and the code must move to the production pipeline path before this PR can be reconsidered.

target.title: fix(coalescer): merge buffered text into media payload instead of splitting
target.body:
When an assistant response contains both text and a `MEDIA:` attachment, the block reply coalescer flushes them as two separate Discord messages: text first, media second. Discord's API supports sending text + file in a single message, so the coalescer should merge them.

Fixes #86446

## Summary

In `createBlockReplyCoalescer`, when a media payload arrives and the buffer has text, merge the buffered text into the media payload's `text` field (as caption) instead of flushing the buffer first. Media-only payloads (no buffered text) pass through unchanged.

## Verification

```bash
pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts
# 69 tests passed (67 existing + 2 new, 1 updated)
pnpm check:changed
# 0 warnings, 0 errors, 0 import cycles
```

Behavior addressed: text + media assistant replies send as two Discord messages instead of one
Real environment tested: local macOS, Node 26
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts
Evidence after fix: 69/69 tests pass; updated test verifies text+media coalesce into single flush
Observed result after fix: buffered text merges into media payload as caption text in one flush
What was not tested: live Discord channel (no bot token available locally)
